title: Judith Ousting Minotaur
description: This work reimagines Judith Beheading Holofernes through a playful and contemporary lens. Drawing from the dramatic intensity and chiaroscuro of Caravaggio, the piece transforms the original narrative into a whimsical yet layered allegory of revelation and disguise. In this reinterpretation, Judith's decisive act is prompted not by political or heroic necessity, but by an unexpected and almost absurd chain of discovery: a domestic moment in which her cat captures a mouse, the mouse uncovers sunflower seeds, and the seeds ultimately expose her lover as a Minotaur in disguise. The intrusion of the uncanny into the intimate space reframes the scene, merging humor with tension. By juxtaposing Baroque drama with surreal storytelling, the work explores themes of perception, hidden identity, and the fragile boundary between the ordinary and the fantastical.

title: Violin Performance
description: This painting of a violin was created as part of a visual performance presented by the artist, Sarah, in collaboration with several colleagues for an orchestral reception and pre-show preceding the formal concert. The instrument depicted belongs to one of her fellow performers, grounding the work in a personal and collaborative context. As a still life, the piece reflects the evolving bond between two individuals whose distinct beginnings converge through their shared dedication to the arts. It speaks to the unifying power of creative practice, illustrating how artistic spaces foster connection, mutual understanding, and parallel journeys.

title: Desk Still Life
description: This painting presents a focused study of the corner of a desk, examining the interplay of light, form, and spatial composition within a familiar setting. The work employs the Verdaccio technique, utilizing a green-toned underpainting to establish a cohesive value structure and sense of depth. Subsequent layers are developed through glazing, allowing for subtle modulation of tone and color while preserving the integrity of the underlying forms.

title: Park Still Life
description: This painting presents a close-up study of a weathered playground structure, drawing attention to surface texture, form, and the passage of time. The work employs the Grisaille technique, using a monochromatic range of black and white values to establish a strong underpainting that defines depth, contrast, and structural clarity. Layers of glazing are applied over this foundation, enriching tonal variation and subtly building color while preserving the integrity of the underlying value structure.

title: Color Theory Study
description: This painting was developed as part of a coursework assignment completed by Sarah at the University of Central Florida. The composition juxtaposes natural, organic forms—such as flowers—with the angular, structural geometry of man-made architecture, creating a visual dialogue between the organic and the constructed. The work emphasizes the application of color theory, integrating analogous, monochromatic, tertiary, and complementary color harmonies to achieve a cohesive and visually engaging composition.

title: Self Portrait
description: This work is a self-portrait by the artist, Sarah, exploring the expressive potential of light and form. The use of harsh, directional lighting creates a heightened sense of drama while emphasizing the structural planes of the face. Rooted in direct observation, the piece prioritizes what is visually perceived over preconceived notions, resulting in a study that is both disciplined and perceptually driven.

title: Bones Still Life
description: This work is a study of both human and animal anatomy, focusing on structure, proportion, and comparative form. The selective use of purple introduces a subtle contrast, enhancing visual interest while drawing attention to key areas within the composition.

title: Portrait Study
description: This work is an academic portrait study of a live model, emphasizing careful observation and technical precision. Through the application of color theory and pastel drawing techniques, the artist builds nuanced, lifelike skin tones, creating a sense of depth and presence on the paper.

title: Moving Figure
description: This work is a dynamic figure study capturing a model in motion, with poses changing at twenty-minute intervals. The resulting composition reads as a sequential, animation-like drawing, conveying movement and the passage of time through repeated forms. The use of color serves to articulate and define the contours of the body, enhancing the sense of structure and continuity across each pose.

title: Model and Still Life
description: This drawing originates from a live figure session incorporating still-life elements, including human and animal skeletons. The composition emphasizes accuracy in the rendering of each figure, while the centrally positioned skeleton is developed with greater definition to guide the viewer’s attention. This contrast in emphasis establishes a clear focal point and reinforces the structural relationships within the scene.

title: Morning Coffee
description: This photograph was captured during a morning coffee outing in Huế, Vietnam, documenting a quiet, everyday moment. Bright sunlight filters across plants and motorbikes, creating a dynamic interplay of light and shadow that animates the scene. The warm, earthy tones of the environment evoke a sense of calm, reflecting the atmosphere of a peaceful outdoor coffee experience.

title: City View Series-22
description: This photograph is part of a larger series documenting urban landscapes across Vietnam. The project began during Sarah’s travels through her family’s home country, where she spent a month exploring regions in the north, central, and southern parts of the country. The series reflects her observations and experiences, capturing the character and visual richness of the cities she encountered, while also serving as a personal exploration of place, culture, and connection.

title: Tranquil Meal
description: This photograph captures a quiet, contemplative moment of a peaceful, unhurried lunch. The architecture of the restaurant subtly frames the composition, guiding the viewer’s eye through the scene. Earthy green tones contrast with the red brick structure in the background, creating a balanced interplay of color and enhancing the sense of calm within the environment.

title: Botanical Study
description: Close-up macro photography of dried flowers and foliage. Shallow depth of field and natural window light turn organic textures into near-abstract compositions.
